mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that has been flat or gradually declining relative to its 6-month trailing average signals stable-to-easing financial conditions, which lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and produces persistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-trend periods do not show significantly higher mean returns than negative-trend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early slowdown
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return difference versus non-triggered periods is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) across a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals robust near-term GDP momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone reliably predicts returns because retail strength can offset weak housing and vice versa.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration signal fails to outperform either univariate signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals continued factory-sector strength that supports near-term risk appetite, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week; the effect is expected to be weak or reversed in early-cycle or recessionary regimes where INDPRO gains reflect inventory overshoot.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to produce mean 5-day forward returns above the unconditional baseline by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) when restricted to late-cycle periods, or the sign flips (INDPRO up but forward returns systematically negative) in more than 50% of late-cycle instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds a threshold consistent with above-target inflation while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, markets price in a higher probability of continued restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as valuation multiples compress.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: During triggered periods, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC conditional on the trigger is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) and a simultaneous uptick in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signals a genuine labor market turning point rather than noise in either series alone, and this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as recession risk repricing occurs.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rising
failure_condition: Joint signal's Rank IC does not exceed the best univariate PAYEMS or UNRATE signal by a meaningful margin, or produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects durable aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter through sustained upward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters fail to show significantly higher mean 60-day forward SPX returns than negative growth quarters (p > 0.05) over a 15-year sample, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in housing starts (HOUST) combined with continued elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that rate hikes are transmitting effectively into rate-sensitive sectors, foreshadowing broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week; this joint pattern is not meaningful outside tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND HOUST declining 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: In triggered tightening-cycle episodes, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint signal underperforms the univariate FEDFUNDS-only signal in Rank IC over the same sample.
